hero_tagline: We know choosing the perfect holiday park for your family near 1770 can feel overwhelming, so we're here to help you find accommodation that matches your kids' ages and your travel style.
heading: Best Family Holiday & Caravan Parks in Seventeen Seventy
hero_intro:
Planning a family trip to 1770? We compared the family holiday and caravan park options in Seventeen Seventy so you can quickly find the right base for your kids, budget and travel style.
1770 is a one-road village on a headland where the sun sets over the water, the creek runs calm and shallow, and the biggest decision of the day is beach or boardwalk.
local_knowledge: The 1770 Camping Ground's waterfront position on the calm side of the headland is the drawcard, shallow, protected water where young kids paddle safely while parents watch from camp. This is one of the few east coast spots where the sun sets over the sea, and the headland at Round Hill draws a small crowd nightly. The LARC amphibious vehicle tours up the coast to Bustard Head lighthouse are a genuine kid magnet, part boat, part bus, all novelty. Traveller's Rest brings boardwalks and bush tucker signage for nature-minded families. Surf, shops and fuel live at Agnes Water ten minutes back down the road.

if_we_were_booking.label: Waterfront camping
if_we_were_booking.text: We'd choose 1770 Camping Ground for the absolute waterfront sites on calm, shallow water, accepting rustic facilities as the price of the position.

if_we_were_booking.label: Nature and comfort
if_we_were_booking.text: We'd choose Traveller's Rest 1770 for the boardwalk, birdlife and tidier amenities, driving five minutes to the water.

if_we_were_booking.label: Our tip
if_we_were_booking.text: Book a LARC tour. The big pink amphibious vehicle drives off the beach straight into the water on its way to the lighthouse, and no child has ever been unimpressed by that.

faq.question: Is 1770 good for young children?
faq.answer: Yes, the protected side of the headland offers calm, shallow paddling water, and the village pace suits families who want quiet.

faq.question: What is the LARC tour?
faq.answer: An amphibious vehicle tour that drives along beaches and through the water to Bustard Head lighthouse. It's the region's signature family experience.

faq.question: Which park should families choose?
faq.answer: 1770 Camping Ground for waterfront sites and calm-water paddling, or Traveller's Rest for nature boardwalks and neater facilities a short drive from the water.

faq.question: Why is the sunset famous here?
faq.answer: The headland faces west over the water, rare on the east coast, so the sun sets into the sea. The Round Hill headland gathers a small crowd nightly.

faq.question: Can you get to the reef from 1770?
faq.answer: Yes, Lady Musgrave Island day trips leave from the 1770 marina with calm lagoon snorkelling suited to families.

faq.question: Where do you buy supplies?
faq.answer: Agnes Water, ten minutes away, has the supermarket, fuel and shops. 1770 itself is a cafe, a marina and not much else, deliberately.

faq.question: What are the best free family activities?
faq.answer: Paddling the calm foreshore, the headland walk at Round Hill, the paperbark boardwalk and crab-spotting in the mangroves at low tide.

faq.question: When is the best time to visit?
faq.answer: April to October for mild weather. Winter sunsets over the water are the postcard version.

faq.question: What should families do when it rains?
faq.answer: Very little is undercover. A shower means the cafe; settled rain means Agnes Water's shops or an early LARC booking when it clears.

faq.question: How far ahead should you book?
faq.answer: The camping ground's waterfront sites are the region's hottest ticket and book out months ahead for holidays.
